metadata.summary: Playwright E2E validates critical user journeys. Page Object Model, data-testid selectors only, API-based seeding, less than 50 critical tests. Smoke suite gates production deploys.

content.title: End-to-End Testing with Playwright
content.summary: End-to-end testing with Playwright validates complete user journeys through the full application stack. Core rules: use Page Object Model (one POM per route), select only via `data-testid` or ARIA roles, seed/tear-down test data via API never via UI, and limit the critical-path suite to less than 50 tests. E2E covers journeys; unit and integration tests cover logic.

why.p: E2E tests are the only layer that validates the rendered DOM, browser engine behavior, and real backend integration together. They are 10-20x slower than unit tests and are the most expensive to maintain, so they must be reserved for critical user journeys (signup, checkout, payment) and smoke gates on deploys, not for logic branches already covered by cheaper tests.

list.item: Critical revenue/auth paths (login, signup, checkout, payment) with a real browser + real backend.
Smoke suite gating production deploys — 5-15 fast tests, run post-deploy, roll back on failure.
Cross-browser parity where engine-specific bugs can't be caught by unit tests.
Visual-regression baselines for marketing pages.
LLM-generated UI changes that need a rendered DOM gate.
Business-rule branches already covered by unit or integration tests — duplicating them adds cost with no signal.
Input validation edge cases — drive via API tests; the browser layer is wasted cycles.
Performance benchmarking — Playwright measurement overhead distorts numbers; use Lighthouse CI / k6.
Pre-MVP UIs changing shape weekly — fixture/selector churn outpaces value.
Third-party flows without a sandbox mode — non-deterministic, cannot be cleaned up.

rule.statement: E2E tests cover complete user journeys only — not individual component behavior or API logic.
rule.rationale: E2E tests are 10-20x slower than unit tests. Testing logic already covered at cheaper layers wastes CI budget and creates maintenance cost without adding signal.

rule.statement: Limit the critical-path E2E suite to less than 50 tests. Gate non-critical tests to a nightly run.
rule.rationale: Suites beyond 50 tests routinely exceed 30 minutes in CI, causing teams to skip or disable them. A tight budget forces selection of only the most valuable journeys.

rule.statement: Use only `getByTestId()`, `getByRole()`, or `getByLabel()` locators. Never use CSS selectors, IDs, or XPath.
rule.rationale: CSS selectors break on any style refactor. `data-testid` attributes are stable and signal intent to developers that the element is tested. `getByRole` aligns tests with accessibility expectations.

rule.statement: Never use `page.waitForTimeout()` or hard-coded sleeps. Use Playwright's built-in auto-wait via `expect(locator).toBeVisible()` or explicit network/navigation waits.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded waits are the primary source of flakiness in E2E suites. Playwright auto-waits for actionability; explicit assertions wait for the right state.

rule.statement: Each test must seed its own data via API in `beforeEach` and clean up in `afterEach`. Never rely on data left by another test.
rule.rationale: Test-order dependencies cause cascading failures that are difficult to diagnose. API-based seeding with timestamped unique data prevents `unique_email` violations.

rule.statement: Reuse `storageState` for authenticated sessions — log in once in global setup; use saved cookies for the rest of the suite.
rule.rationale: Logging in via UI per test adds 2-5 seconds each. Saved auth state reduces suite time by 30-50% on auth-heavy applications.

rule.statement: Create one Page Object class per route. Name methods after user intent, not DOM events: `login()` not `clickSubmitButton()`.
rule.rationale: Intent-based names make test specs read like user stories. DOM-event names couple the test to the implementation and break on any UI restructure.

example.title: LoginPage with intent-based methods
example.note: All locators use `getByTestId`. `goto()` waits for page readiness. Methods are named after user actions.

example.title: Fixtures for shared state
example.note: Fixtures compose: `authenticatedPage` depends on `testUser`. Cleanup runs automatically after each test.

rule.statement: Create and clean up test data via API endpoints in beforeEach/afterEach, never through the UI.
rule.rationale: UI-based seeding tests something other than the actual test scenario and is 5-10x slower. API seeding is deterministic and isolated.

example.title: API-based test user creation
example.note: Timestamped + random email prevents unique_email collisions on parallel test runs.

rule.statement: Mock all third-party API calls at the network boundary using `page.route()`. Never depend on live SaaS in CI.
rule.rationale: Real third-party calls add 1-2% flake rate from network variability. Stripe, Auth0, and similar services have sandbox modes but even those add latency and can have outages.

example.title: Mocking Stripe payment API

antipattern.description: `page.waitForTimeout(5000)` or any hard-coded sleep instead of element/navigation waits.
antipattern.reason: Arbitrary sleeps are the primary source of flakiness — too short on slow CI, too long locally. Playwright auto-waits for actionability; use `expect(locator).toBeVisible()` instead.

antipattern.description: CSS selectors or dynamic IDs (`#submit-btn-1234`) as locators.
antipattern.reason: CSS selectors break on any style refactor; dynamic IDs change on every render. Only `data-testid` and ARIA-based locators (`getByRole`, `getByLabel`) are stable.

antipattern.description: Tests sharing a single user account across parallel workers.
antipattern.reason: Concurrent password changes, profile updates, or session mutations from parallel tests cause cascading failures. Use per-worker or per-test accounts.

antipattern.description: Visual snapshots committed from a macOS runner, diffed against a Linux CI runner.
antipattern.reason: Anti-aliasing and font rendering differ across OS. Visual snapshots must be captured and compared on the same OS. Force snapshot updates from one Linux runner only.

antipattern.description: `page.locator(...).click({ force: true })` to bypass actionability checks.
antipattern.reason: Force bypasses Playwright's visibility and interactability checks, hiding real UI bugs (overlapping elements, disabled states). It is always a symptom of a real problem to fix.

antipattern.description: E2E suite growing beyond 200 tests with no nightly/critical distinction.
antipattern.reason: Above 50 critical-path tests the suite exceeds 30 minutes; teams skip it. Enforce a budget; promote logic tests to unit/integration when possible.

antipattern.description: Trace ZIP files committed to git or stored in public CI artifacts without scrubbing.
antipattern.reason: Playwright traces capture network requests including auth tokens. Scrub via `playwright.config.ts` `recordHar` filters or add artifacts to `.gitignore`.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Decompose E2E into three phases handled by separate subagents: (1) a journey-mapper subagent reads product specs (spec.md, Linear ticket, Jira) and emits a list of user journeys with success criteria; (2) a page-object-author subagent writes/updates page objects in `e2e/pages/` (one POM per route, methods named after user actions); (3) a spec-writer subagent generates `.spec.ts` files using fixtures + POM + API helpers. Run `npx playwright test --reporter=list` and feed failures back to a flake-triage subagent that classifies each failure as: real bug / selector drift / timing / data pollution / infra. Persist artifacts (`test-results/`, traces, videos) for human review on first failure; auto-retry only on infra class.

Recommended subagents: faion-sdd-executor-agent (quality gate runs `npm run test:e2e` after impl tasks; blocks merge if smoke fails), a purpose-built playwright-trace-agent (consumes `trace.zip`, reports the failing locator + last network call + DOM diff vs prior run), a journey-mapper-agent (converts BA acceptance criteria into a list of `test('...', ...)` stubs and stable `data-testid` proposals), password-scrubber-agent (scrub `.env.test`, `playwright.config.ts`, fixture credentials before commit).

list.item: One POM per route. `LoginPage` → `/login`, `DashboardPage` → `/dashboard`. Methods named after user intent (`login()`, `logout()`), not DOM events (`clickButton()`).
`data-testid` is the only stable selector. Discipline at code-review time; agents otherwise default to text matchers that break on i18n.
Seed/tear-down via API, never via UI. UI seeding is slow + flaky and tests something other than the SUT.
Reuse `storageState` for authenticated tests. Log in once in global setup; saved cookies for the rest. Refresh if 401 leaks.
Tag tests (`@smoke`, `@critical`, `@nightly`). Drives deploy gates.
Mock third-party calls at the network boundary (`page.route('https://api.stripe.com/**', ...)`); never depend on live SaaS in CI.
Trace + video on first retry only. Cheaper CI, still recoverable on failure.
Pin browser version in CI (`npx playwright install --with-deps chromium@1.40.0`) to match local snapshot baselines.
Fail fast on console errors. Hook `page.on('pageerror', ...)` and `page.on('console', ...)` to surface JS errors as test failures.
Limit suite to less than 50 critical-path tests + a separate nightly suite. E2E is for journeys, not coverage.

section.name: cli-tools
section.p: npx playwright test — run E2E suite. npx playwright codegen — record interactions → starter spec. npx playwright show-trace — inspect failed test traces. npx playwright install — install browser binaries. Cypress — alt runner; better dev DX, single-tab. WebdriverIO — cross-browser, mobile-friendly. lighthouse-ci — perf E2E gate. axe-playwright — a11y inside E2E. Percy / chromatic — visual regression as a service. BrowserStack / Sauce Labs — real-device cloud. Microsoft Playwright Service — hosted Playwright workers. Cypress Cloud — test recording + parallel orchestration. Allure / ReportPortal — rich reporting + ML-based flaky test detection. Datadog Synthetics — production smoke tests. Checkly — Playwright-as-a-monitor; cron-driven prod E2E.

template.purpose: Multi-browser config with retries, reporters, baseURL, webServer.
POM skeleton with constructor, goto, action, and assertion methods.
Deploy gate script running @smoke-tagged tests against a staging URL.
